sanskrit: श्वशुरान्सुहृदश्चैव सेनयोरुभयोरपि । तान्समीक्ष्य स कौन्तेयः सर्वान्बन्धूनवस्थितान् ॥
hindiTranslation: अपनीअपनी जगह पर स्थित उन सम्पूर्ण बान्धवों को देखकर वे कुन्तीनन्दन अर्जुन अत्यन्त कायरता से युक्त होकर विषाद करते हुए ये वचन बोले ॥
englishTranslation: Seeing all his relatives standing there, Arjuna sank into grief and spoke in fear.
essence: The battle becomes unbearable when every side is family.
speaker: Sanjaya
simpleMeaning: Arjuna sees every kind of relative on both sides of the battlefield and is overwhelmed with grief.
simpleInsight: Seeing familiar faces can make a fight feel unbearable.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield of Kurukshetra, Arjuna looks again and sees not strangers, but fathers-in-law, friends, and relatives standing in both armies. That sight deepens his collapse and prepares the words of despair that follow.
modernRelevance: You open a family group chat and realize the conflict has names and faces. The issue is no longer abstract, and your stomach drops.
translationLiteral: श्वशुरान् (fathers-in-law) / सुहृदः (well-wishers) / च (and) / एव (indeed) / सेनयोः (of the armies) / उभयोः (of both) / अपि (also) / तान् (those) / समीक्ष्य (having seen) / सः (he) / कौन्तेयः (son of Kunti) / सर्वान् (all) / बन्धून् (relatives) / अवस्थितान् (standing)
